title: asclepias eastwoodiana
intro: Asclepias eastwoodiana, also known as Eastwood's milkweed or Eastwood's butterfly milkweed, is a species of milkweed native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is a member of the Apocynaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. Asclepias eastwoodiana contains cardenolides, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and as a treatment for cancer. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
